content.problemStatement:
Chef c8kbf has an unrooted, weighted tree with NNN nodes, where vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge with weight www.

The **value** of the simple path from xxx to yyy is defined as the bitwise XOR of the weights of the edges it consists of.

Recall that a simple path is a path that does not have repeating vertices.

Help Chef c8kbf find any four vertices a,b,c,da, b, c, da,b,c,d, such that:


a<ba \lt ba<b and c<dc \lt dc<d;
The **value** of the simple path from aaa to bbb is equal to that of the path from ccc to ddd;
The pairs (a,b)(a, b)(a,b) and (c,d)(c, d)(c,d) are different. In other words, a≠ca \neq ca=c **or** b≠db \neq db=d.

Note that the four vertices do not have to be all distinct. For example, it is allowed for aaa and ccc to be the same if bbb and ddd are different.
If no four vertices satisfy such a property, report so. If there are multiple answers, output any one of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input. For each test case,

The first line contains an integer NNN, the number of vertices.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines contain three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​, and wiw_iwi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ with weight wiw_iwi​. It is guaranteed that these edges will form a tree.




Output Format
For each test case, output four space-separated integers aaa, bbb, ccc, and ddd on a new line, representing the four vertices. If there are multiple answers, output any one of them.


If no four vertices satisfy the property, output −1-1−1 on a new line instead.
constraints: ui≠viu_i \neq v_iui​=vi​ for each 1≤i≤N−11 \leq i \leq N-11≤i≤N−1.
It is guaranteed that the given edges will form a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The value of the path between vertices 111 and 444 is 1⊕3=21\oplus 3 = 21⊕3=2. The path between vertices 333 and 222 only has one edge, so the value is also 222. Note that 222 333 111 444 or 222 444 111 333 would be other acceptable answers.
Test case 222: No four vertices satisfy the property.
Test case 333: Note that a=ca = ca=c for the output of this case, which is allowed.
